controls.name: Broken Access Control
controls.official_text: Access control enforces policy such that users cannot act outside of their intended permissions. Failures typically lead to unauthorized information disclosure, modification, or destruction of all data or performing a business function outside the user's limits.

controls.name: Cryptographic Failures
controls.official_text: The first thing is to determine the protection needs of data in transit and at rest. Passwords, credit card numbers, health records, personal information, and business secrets require extra protection.

checks.description: TLS/HTTPS enforced for data in transit

evidence: ✓ TLS/HTTPS enforced for data in transit

controls.name: Injection
controls.official_text: An application is vulnerable to attack when user-supplied data is not validated, filtered, or sanitized by the application.

controls.name: Insecure Design
controls.official_text: Insecure design is a broad category representing different weaknesses, expressed as missing or ineffective control design.

controls.name: Security Misconfiguration
controls.official_text: The application might be vulnerable if the application is missing appropriate security hardening across any part of the application stack.

controls.name: Vulnerable and Outdated Components
controls.official_text: You are likely vulnerable if you do not know the versions of all components you use. Software is vulnerable, unsupported, or out of date.

evidence: ✓ Checked via npm audit - see Dependencies tab

controls.name: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
controls.official_text: This category is to help detect, escalate, and respond to active breaches. Without logging and monitoring, breaches cannot be detected.

controls.name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
controls.official_text: SSRF flaws occur whenever a web application is fetching a remote resource without validating the user-supplied URL.

checks.description: No unvalidated URL fetching from user input

nist.note: This scanner checks CODE-RELATED controls only. Organizational/process controls require manual assessment.

functions.name: PROTECT
functions.description: Develop and implement appropriate safeguards to ensure delivery of critical services.

categories.name: Identity Management, Authentication and Access Control
categories.official_text: Access to physical and logical assets and associated facilities is limited to authorized users, processes, and devices, and is managed consistent with the assessed risk of unauthorized access to authorized activities and transactions.

controls.description: Identities and credentials are issued, managed, verified, revoked, and audited
Access permissions and authorizations are managed with least privilege

categories.name: Data Security
categories.official_text: Information and records (data) are managed consistent with the organization's risk strategy to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information.

controls.description: Protections against data leaks are implemented

categories.name: Information Protection Processes and Procedures
categories.official_text: Security policies, processes, and procedures are maintained and used to manage protection of information systems and assets.

controls.description: Configuration of systems incorporates security principles
Response and recovery plans are in place
A vulnerability management plan is developed and implemented

categories.name: Protective Technology
categories.official_text: Technical security solutions are managed to ensure the security and resilience of systems and assets, consistent with related policies, procedures, and agreements.

controls.description: Audit/log records are determined, documented, implemented, and reviewed
The principle of least functionality is incorporated
Communications and control networks are protected

functions.name: DETECT
functions.description: Develop and implement appropriate activities to identify the occurrence of a cybersecurity event.

categories.name: Anomalies and Events
categories.official_text: Anomalous activity is detected and the potential impact of events is understood.

controls.description: A baseline of network operations and expected data flows is established
Event data are collected and correlated from multiple sources

categories.name: Security Continuous Monitoring
categories.official_text: The information system and assets are monitored to identify cybersecurity events and verify the effectiveness of protective measures.

controls.description: The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
Monitoring for unauthorized personnel, connections, devices, and software is performed

categories.name: Detection Processes
categories.official_text: Detection processes and procedures are maintained and tested to ensure awareness of anomalous events.

excluded.name: IDENTIFY
excluded.reason: Organizational process - asset inventory, risk assessment, governance

excluded.name: RESPOND
excluded.reason: Organizational process - incident response planning, communications

csa.note: This scanner checks CODE-RELATED controls only. Infrastructure and organizational controls require manual assessment.

domains.name: Application & Interface Security
domains.description: Controls that address the security of application interfaces and software development.

controls.name: Application Security Policy
controls.official_text: Policies and procedures shall be established, and supporting business processes and technical measures implemented, to ensure protection of application services throughout its lifecycle.

controls.name: Application Security Baseline Requirements
controls.official_text: Baseline requirements shall be established for securing different types of applications (e.g., web, mobile, cloud-native).

controls.name: Application Security Metrics
controls.official_text: Technical security metrics shall be defined, implemented, and reported.

controls.name: Secure Application Design and Development
controls.official_text: Applications shall be designed, developed, deployed, and tested in accordance with industry-accepted secure design and development practices.

controls.name: Application Vulnerability Remediation
controls.official_text: Technical vulnerabilities in applications shall be corrected in a timely manner.

domains.name: Data Security & Information Lifecycle Management
domains.description: Controls that address data security throughout its lifecycle.

controls.name: Data Security Policy
controls.official_text: Policies and procedures shall be established for the classification, protection, and handling of data throughout its lifecycle.

controls.name: Data Classification
controls.official_text: Data and assets shall be classified according to their business value and criticality.

controls.name: Data Flow Documentation
controls.official_text: Data flows shall be documented and maintained.

controls.name: Encryption and Key Management
controls.official_text: Data shall be protected using encryption throughout its lifecycle where technically feasible and legally permitted.

domains.name: Identity & Access Management
domains.description: Controls that address identity management and access control.

controls.name: Identity and Access Management Policy
controls.official_text: Identity and access management policies and procedures shall be established.

controls.name: Strong Authentication
controls.official_text: Strong authentication shall be implemented for access to systems and applications.

controls.name: Separation of Duties
controls.official_text: Segregation of duties shall be implemented within the identity and access management processes.

controls.name: User Access Provisioning
controls.official_text: User access provisioning shall follow the principles of least privilege.

controls.name: User Access Review
controls.official_text: User access shall be reviewed and revalidated for appropriateness on a regular basis.

controls.name: Network Security
controls.official_text: Networks shall be designed, developed, deployed, and configured to restrict unauthorized traffic.

controls.name: Network Defense
controls.official_text: Network-based intrusion detection and prevention systems shall be deployed to protect network traffic.

domains.name: Security Incident Management, E-Discovery & Cloud Forensics
domains.description: Controls that address security incident management and forensics.

controls.name: Incident Management Policy
controls.official_text: Policies and procedures shall be established to triage security incidents and preserve forensic evidence.

controls.name: Incident Response Plans
controls.official_text: Incident response plans shall include identification of roles and responsibilities.

controls.name: Incident Response Testing
controls.official_text: Incident response capabilities shall be tested on a planned basis.

domains.name: Logging and Monitoring
domains.description: Controls that address logging and monitoring.

controls.name: Logging and Monitoring Policy
controls.official_text: Logging and monitoring policies and procedures shall be established and maintained.

controls.name: Security Monitoring and Alerting
controls.official_text: Security-related events shall be identified and logged.

controls.name: Audit Logging
controls.official_text: Audit logs shall be collected, protected, and retained.

excluded.name: Cryptography, Encryption & Key Management
excluded.reason: Partially covered in DSI-07, infrastructure controls require manual assessment

excluded.name: Supply Chain Management, Transparency, and Accountability
Threat & Vulnerability Management
excluded.reason: Partially covered via npm audit, full scope requires manual assessment

excluded.name: Universal Endpoint Management
excluded.reason: Endpoint management - not application code related
